paper_title: Opportunity analysis of agriculture products in Sri Lanka: Measuring markets and feasibility
research_answer: The US market is notably dynamic — most selected products show high or very high growth rates there between 2012–2016, making it a broadly favorable destination by growth metrics. The products with the very largest US market sizes are Shrimp, Tomatoes, Bell Pepper, Cut Flowers, and Plantains & Bananas. Bell Pepper is particularly notable as most of its global market growth is actually being driven by the US. Shrimp combines a very large US market with considerable growth, making it arguably the strongest single opportunity. Tomatoes have a large US market but show only moderate growth there (and are shrinking in the EU). The US market's broad growth momentum across most product categories — unlike the more selective EU market — makes it an attractive and relatively lower-risk diversification destination for agricultural exporters.
supporting_quotes: Most of the products have high or very high growth rates in the US market.
In particular, Shrimp, Tomatoes, Bell Pepper, Cut Flowers, and Plantains & Bananas have very large markets in the US.
Bell Pepper: Moderate market sizes in EU, US and globally; most growth coming from US
Tomatoes: Large world market but low growth (moderate in US and shrinking in EU)

data_points.value: high or very high for most products

data_points.product: Shrimp, Tomatoes, Bell Pepper, Cut Flowers, Plantains & Bananas
